Title: Histone H3 lysine 27 trimethylation suppresses jasmonate biosynthesis and signaling to affect male fertility under high temperature in cotton

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polycomb-associated H3K27 trimethylation machinery target state
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains anther dehiscence, pollen fertility, and male thermotolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced H3K27me3 deposition increases JA biosynthesis gene expression, JA content, and male fertility under HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical inhibition with GSK343; ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; JA quantification; anther and pollen phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Global H3K27me3 decreases in tolerant 84021 but increases in sensitive H05 under high temperature, especially at ADS. 2. Chemical suppression of H3K27me3 deposition by GSK343 lowers H3K27me3 on JA-related genes, raises JA content, and rescues male fertility in H05 under HT. 3. The study identifies H3K27me3 state itself as a decisive epigenetic determinant of JA homeostasis and thermotolerant male fertility.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: This is the central epigenetic mechanism studied: promoter H3K27me3 represses JA biosynthesis/signaling genes under high temperature
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter epigenome editing or controlled inhibition of H3K27me3 deposition could be used to improve cotton heat tolerance during reproduction
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad reduction of H3K27me3 may cause pleiotropic transcriptional changes beyond JA pathway
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common gene/process-level core factor because the paper’s primary causal focus is epigenetic regulation of nutrient/hormone metabolism rather than a single writer gene; no specific writer/eraser gene was functionally validated as core in Results

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway, allene oxide formation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrates fatty acid hydroperoxide to an unstable allene oxide in the jasmonic acid biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for anther development, anther dehiscence, pollen fertility, and high-temperature tolerance of male reproduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anthers, especially at anther dehiscence stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At the anther dehiscence stage, expression is higher in HT-tolerant line 84021 under high temperature than under normal temperature; not substantially changed in H05
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function decreases JA biosynthesis and causes male sterility with indehiscent anthers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; locus-specific expression/H3K27me3 comparison; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutant analysis; pollen/anther phenotype observation; JA measurement; chemical H3K27me3 inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H3K27me3 at the GhAOS locus decreases in the HT-tolerant line 84021 under high temperature but not substantially in H05. 2. GhAOS expression increases in 84021 under high temperature, consistent with release from H3K27me3 repression. 3. CRISPR-Cas9 Ghaos mutants show anther indehiscence and male sterility even under normal temperature, demonstrating that GhAOS is essential for JA-mediated male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in JA biosynthesis for male fertility under heat; explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature; exogenous H3K27me3 inhibitor GSK343
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-associated H3K27me3 represses GhAOS; H3K27me3 decreases under HT in tolerant 84021 but remains comparatively high in sensitive H05; GSK343 reduces H3K27me3 and increases GhAOS expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving cotton male fertility under heat by promoter epigenome editing, reducing H3K27me3 deposition, or stacking with other JA biosynthetic genes; knockout should be avoided because it causes sterility
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe male sterility/anther indehiscence; broader JA perturbation may affect defense-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene function was validated in cultivar Jin668 by CRISPR, while epigenetic/expression comparisons were mainly in H05 and 84021 under NT/HT

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway, cyclization step to OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes the unstable allene oxide intermediate to form cis-(+)-oxophytodienoic acid in jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading directly to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for anther development, anther dehiscence, pollen fertility, and heat tolerance of male reproduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anthers, especially at anther dehiscence stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At the anther dehiscence stage, expression is higher in HT-tolerant line 84021 under high temperature than under normal temperature; not substantially changed in H05
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis and maintains male fertility; reduced GhAOC2 function causes anther indehiscence and male sterility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; locus-specific expression/H3K27me3 comparison; mutant analysis; pollen/anther phenotype observation; chemical H3K27me3 inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H3K27me3 at the GhAOC2 locus is lower in 84021 under high temperature but not substantially changed in H05. 2. GhAOC2 expression is higher in 84021 under high temperature, consistent with reduced repression. 3. Ghaoc2 mutants display non-dehiscent anthers and male sterility, confirming its essential role in JA-mediated fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key biosynthetic step for JA production affecting male fertility under heat; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature; exogenous H3K27me3 inhibitor GSK343
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-associated H3K27me3 represses GhAOC2; reduction of H3K27me3 by GSK343 increases expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving heat-resilient male fertility via epigenetic de-repression or pathway stacking with GhAOS; knockout should be avoided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes male sterility; manipulation of JA pathway may alter other JA-dependent processes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although mutant material was from previous work, this paper integrates its phenotype with H3K27me3-mediated regulation under heat

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor protein with TIFY and Jas domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of jasmonate-responsive transcription factors/signaling outputs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports anther development and fertility under heat through proper jasmonate signaling homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At the ADS under HT, GhJAZ1 expression is lower in H05 and higher in 84021 relative to comparative patterns noted in the paper; overexpression tested in YZ1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altered GhJAZ1 expression perturbs JA signaling and causes partial anther indehiscence/male sterility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; overexpression analysis; anther dehiscence scoring; comparison with RNAi lines from previous study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhJAZ1 shows differential H3K27me3 and expression patterns between H05 and 84021 under high temperature. 2. GhJAZ1 overexpression lines display partially indehiscent anthers, indicating that excessive repression of JA signaling disrupts male fertility. 3. JAZ1 RNAi lines dehisce normally under normal temperature, suggesting functional redundancy among homologs.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses jasmonate-responsive transcriptional outputs; precise direct downstream targets not identified in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate pathway status; high temperature-associated H3K27me3 dynamics
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator of JA signaling affecting anther dehiscence and fertility rather than a master determinant of JA content
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of jasmonate signaling negative-feedback architecture, generally as JAZ repressor; not directly dissected here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts in JAZ repressor complexes with transcription factors; specific complex members not experimentally tested in this study
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: GhJAZ1 locus carries H3K27me3; higher H3K27me3 in H05 than in 84021 under HT is associated with lower GhJAZ1 expression in H05
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation requires caution because both excessive repression and insufficient specificity may impair fertility; better used in fine-tuning JA signaling alongside biosynthetic genes rather than strong constitutive overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes partial anther indehiscence; redundancy may reduce predictability of single-gene interventions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although JAZ proteins are classical repressors, the paper treats GhJAZ1 as a JA-related signaling gene whose misexpression affects fertility; direct downstream TFs were not mapped here
